overview.short: Narayangad is a fort in Pune district near to khodad-Gadachiwadi in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: National Centre for Radio Astrophysics
topPlaces.description: The National Centre for Radio Astrophysics (NCRA; Hindi: राष्ट्रीय रेडियो खगोल भौतिकी केन्द्र) is an international research institution in India in the field of radio astronomy is located in the Pune University Campus (just beside IUCAA), is part of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research, Mumbai, India. NCRA has an active research program in many areas of Astronomy and Astrophysics, which includes studies of the Sun, Interplanetary scintillations, pulsars, the Interstellar medium, Active galaxies and cosmology and particularly in the specialized field of Radio Astronomy and Radio instrumentation.

topPlaces.name: Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope
topPlaces.description: The Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), located near Narayangaon, Pune in India, is an array of thirty fully steerable parabolic radio telescopes of 45 metre diameter, observing at metre wavelengths. It is the largest and most sensitive radio telescope array in the world at low frequencies.

topPlaces.name: Yedgaon Dam
topPlaces.description: Yedgaon Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Kukadi river near Junnar, Pune district, in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Kalwadi
topPlaces.description: Kalwadi is a village in Junnar taluka, Pune district, of Maharashtra, India situated adjuscent to the Kukadi River and Yedgaon Dam. The location code or village code of Kalwadi village is 555279.

topPlaces.name: Lauki, Ambegaon
topPlaces.description: Lauki is a village in Ambegaon taluka of Pune District in the state of Maharashtra, India.The village is administrated by a Sarpanch who is an elected representative of village as per constitution of India and Panchayati raj (India). According to the 2011 census, the village has a population of 924 people.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — National Centre for Radio Astrophysics & around

items.note: The National Centre for Radio Astrophysics (NCRA; Hindi: राष्ट्रीय रेडियो खगोल भौतिकी केन्द्र) is an international research institution in India in the field of radio astronomy is located in the Pune University Campus (just beside IUCAA), is part of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research, Mumbai, India. NCRA has an active research program in many areas of Astronomy and Astrophysics, which includes studies of the Sun, Interplanetary scintillations, pulsars, the Interstellar medium, Active galaxies and cosmology and particularly in the specialized field of Radio Astronomy and Radio instrumentation.

The Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT), located near Narayangaon, Pune in India, is an array of thirty fully steerable parabolic radio telescopes of 45 metre diameter, observing at metre wavelengths. It is the largest and most sensitive radio telescope array in the world at low frequencies.
Yedgaon Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Kukadi river near Junnar, Pune district, in the state of Maharashtra in India.
Kalwadi is a village in Junnar taluka, Pune district, of Maharashtra, India situated adjuscent to the Kukadi River and Yedgaon Dam. The location code or village code of Kalwadi village is 555279.

Lauki is a village in Ambegaon taluka of Pune District in the state of Maharashtra, India.The village is administrated by a Sarpanch who is an elected representative of village as per constitution of India and Panchayati raj (India). According to the 2011 census, the village has a population of 924 people.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Narayangad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Narayangad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (60 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (67 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Narayangad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Narayangad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Narayangad?
faq.a: Stays in Narayangad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Narayangad?
faq.a: The top attractions are National Centre for Radio Astrophysics, Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope, Yedgaon Dam, Kalwadi, Lauki, Ambegaon.
